title: Volatile vs non-deterministic separation
statement: Function definitions shall model volatility and non-determinism as separate axes, not synonyms.
notes: Need final taxonomy and examples policy.

title: Host interaction taxonomy
statement: Function definitions shall declare host interaction dependency class (none/workbook/app/environment/external-provider).
notes: Key for reproducible conformance replay.

title: Invalidation trigger declaration
statement: Each function definition shall declare invalidation triggers (T-DEP,T-VOL,T-HOST,T-EXT,T-VERSION).
notes: Needed for reason-code and recalc semantic tests.

title: Aggregate coercion policy boundary
statement: Aggregate function definitions shall distinguish direct-arg coercion from range-scan coercion policies.
notes: Blocked on function-policy decisions for aggregate family.

title: Argument-gap handling policy
statement: Function-definition policy shall specify accepted/rejected/mapped behavior for missing-argument patterns.
notes: Current behavior is build-scoped provisional.

title: Dynamic-array function spill semantics
statement: Function definitions for dynamic-array producers/transformers shall declare spill-shape and blocking expectations.
notes: Links function semantics to table/CF spill mismatch lanes.

title: External reference and open-state policy
statement: Function-policy and formula-policy interpretation shall include workbook-open/link-update state effects for external references.
notes: Same-build baseline exists; cross-build convergence pending.

title: Merge/formula interaction follow-up
statement: Function definitions interacting with merged ranges shall declare behavior expectations or explicit caveats.
notes: New direct merge evidence now available; functional interactions not yet mapped.

title: Operator-as-function inventory
statement: Evaluable operators shall be represented as pseudo-function rows (`OP_*`) in the function-definition lane.
notes: Includes reference operators and trim-reference family.

title: Parse delimiter separation
statement: Parse-only delimiters (for example argument separators) shall be modeled as syntax-layer items, not evaluable function rows.
notes: Locale token profile retained in language concrete rules.

title: Pre-call argument coercion
statement: Function definitions shall declare host-side argument coercion behavior before invocation.
notes: Key for non-interesting-function UDF parity hypothesis.

title: Post-call return adaptation
statement: Function definitions shall declare host-side return adaptation behavior, including array-to-anchor adaptation boundaries.
notes: Spill projection details stay outside this doc.

title: Extended value boundary
statement: Function/value model shall distinguish primary values from extended-value wrappers used at worksheet boundary.
notes: Includes formatting hints, rich error detail, virtual anchor-relative values.

title: XLL UDF registration and context
statement: XLL UDF function definitions shall include registration signature, caller-context, volatility, and reference argument/return semantics.
notes: Source baseline now documented in ../../../Foundation/reference/conformance/excel-worksheet-engine/functions/XLL_SDK_REGISTRATION_AND_TYPES_REFERENCE.md; next step is extracting requirement-level rows.

title: VBA UDF semantics
statement: VBA UDF definitions shall capture scope, argument/reference behavior, and mutation constraints during recalculation.
notes: Open questions retained: Range return semantics and allowed side effects.

title: Automation Add-in UDF semantics
statement: Automation Add-in UDF definitions shall capture COM registration and invocation semantics sufficient for compatibility classification.
notes: Lower priority depth in this phase.

title: JavaScript custom function semantics
statement: JavaScript custom function definitions shall include async/external behavior and extended data-type return implications.
notes: Includes custom/linked data-type style returns.

title: Implicit intersection canonicalization
statement: `@` shall be modeled as canonical operator-function with legacy alias metadata for `SINGLE`/`_xlfn.SINGLE` compatibility contexts.
notes: Need precise compatibility-version mapping.

title: Compatibility-version-scoped definitions
statement: Function definitions shall support workbook-level compatibility-version scoping and explicit divergence metadata.
notes: Axis required in conformance replay matrices.

title: RTD topic lifecycle semantics
statement: RTD semantics shall define topic registration, topic->cell mapping, update-driven invalidation, and disconnection behavior.
notes: Refine with targeted empirical traces for connect/reconnect/disconnect paths.

title: Reference operator pre-call normalization
statement: `#` and structured-reference operands shall have explicit pre-call normalization/dereference policy in function evaluation contract.
notes: Open question: observability by non-interesting functions.

title: Non-interesting function UDF parity hypothesis
statement: Non-interesting functions should be implementable with full fidelity via UDF-style implementations under explicit coercion/reference policies.
notes: Needs systematic differential implementation/test campaign.

title: XLOOKUP reference-return behavior
statement: XLOOKUP reference-output semantics shall be confirmed and classified for host-interaction and determinism axes.
notes: Run targeted empirical matrix and include reference-vs-value output distinctions.

title: Interesting function classification coverage
statement: All interesting functions (tiers 3/4/5) shall have initial axis classification rows, marked provisional where uncertain.
notes: Initial inventory/classification file added for iterative refinement.

title: Function admission vs runtime error contract
statement: Function definitions shall declare parse-admission vs runtime-error boundaries for required arguments, scalar coercion, numeric domain, and array-lift error propagation.
notes: Canonical seed family: SIN(), SIN("asd"), SIN({1,"asd",3}), ASIN(2). Baseline evidence captured in docs/function-lane/FORMULA_ADMISSION_BEHAVIOR_NOTES.md and tools/formula-admission-probe/run-formula-admission-baseline.ps1.

title: PI end-to-end seed slice
statement: `PI()` shall be used as the first cross-lane seed slice with linked contract, Lean obligations, Rust implementation/tests, and correlation ledger entry.
notes: Execution baseline documented in docs/worksets/WORKSET_TUX1000_PI_END_TO_END_SLICE.md and docs/function-lane/FUNCTION_SLICE_PI_CONTRACT_PRELIM.md, with admission-boundary supplement in docs/function-lane/FORMULA_ADMISSION_BEHAVIOR_NOTES.md. Declared thread safety class: `safe_pure`.

title: Floating-point edge normalization characterization
statement: Function/value policy shall explicitly characterize worksheet-visible behavior for IEEE edge values (`-0`, `+/-inf`, NaN variants, subnormals) across formula evaluation, sheet materialization, and reference reuse boundaries.
notes: Baseline closure recorded in docs/function-lane/FLOATING_POINT_EXECUTION_RECORD.md with reproducible suite tooling in tools/fp-probe/run-fp-suite.ps1; promotion to Foundation EMP registry deferred pending multi-build/compat coverage.

title: Value universe and extended-value taxonomy
statement: OxFunc shall define a version-scoped formal value universe, including boundary distinctions across cell content, formula-eval values, reference-like values, and extended-value wrappers.
notes: W3 baseline artifacts include VALUE_UNIVERSE_PRELIM_SPEC/VALUE_UNIVERSE_TAG_TABLE with Rust/Lean mirrors. W7 baseline feed adds text-cap and UTF-16 boundary constraints (including interop truncation edge cases).

title: Coercion primitives and Ref->Val seam
statement: Function semantics shall use explicit conversion/coercion primitives and a formally declared out-of-model reference-resolution seam that maps references to evaluable values under FEC capability contracts.
notes: W4 baseline closure recorded with selected capability-record seam and executable+empirical artifacts in docs/worksets/WORKSET_TUX1000_COERCION_AND_REFERENCE_RESOLUTION_PRIMITIVES.md, docs/function-lane/COERCION_AND_CONVERSION_PRELIM_SPEC.md, docs/function-lane/REF_RESOLUTION_SEAM_OPTIONS.md, docs/function-lane/COERCION_DECISION_TABLE.csv, and docs/function-lane/COERCION_EXECUTION_RECORD.md; includes external/open-state reference row (`CO4-018`) with closed-vs-open observed divergence.

title: ABS full-formality seed
statement: `ABS` shall be formalized with explicit admission/coercion/domain/array-lift/floating-point edge behavior and correlated Lean/Rust/evidence artifacts.
notes: W5 baseline closure recorded in docs/worksets/WORKSET_TUX1000_ABS_FULL_FORMALITY.md with linked contract/formal/runtime/evidence artifacts: docs/function-lane/FUNCTION_SLICE_ABS_CONTRACT_PRELIM.md, docs/function-lane/ABS_SCENARIO_MANIFEST_SEED.csv, docs/function-lane/ABS_PROBE_RUNTIME_REQUIREMENTS.md, docs/function-lane/ABS_EXECUTION_RECORD.md, formal/lean/OxFunc/Functions/Abs.lean, crates/oxfunc_core/src/functions/abs.rs, and tools/abs-probe/*. Empirical suite now enforces dual run labels (`default` + `compat_template`) and includes entrypoint mechanism evidence. Declared thread safety class: `safe_pure`.

title: XMATCH deterministic-quirks closure
statement: `XMATCH` shall be formalized and empirically characterized across mode defaults, search/match variants, coercion, and error propagation to confirm deterministic/nonvolatile classification or retain high-interest status with evidence.
notes: Planned in docs/worksets/WORKSET_TUX1000_XMATCH_DETERMINISTIC_QUIRKS.md; W7 baseline prerequisite satisfied via W7-STR-BL-20260305, with potential refinement from future string replays before final closure; linked to classification review for INTERESTING_FUNCTIONS_INITIAL_CLASSIFICATION.csv.

title: String normalization and comparison characterization
statement: Function/value policy shall explicitly characterize Excel string comparison, normalization, limits, and control/unicode handling across formula, cell, reference, and persistence boundaries.
notes: Baseline closure recorded in STRING_EXECUTION_RECORD.md and STRING_NORMALIZATION_AND_COMPARISON_POLICY_MAP.md with reproducible scenarios in STRING_SCENARIO_MANIFEST_SEED.csv and tools/string-probe/run-string-excel-baseline.ps1. Promotion beyond provisional requires multi-build/compat/locale replay.

title: Function thread safety classification
statement: Function definitions shall declare thread safety class (`safe_pure`, `host_serialized`, `not_thread_safe`) and bind it to reproducible verification evidence.
notes: Axis added to function contracts and executable metadata (Rust/Lean) for PI/ABS seed slices; broader rollout pending.
